title: festuca rubra subsp. nemorum
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. nemorum, also known as wood meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the red fescue plant, which is a type of grass native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and its antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. nemorum include its potential to improve respiratory health, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
